slides.lead: This is a demo (sample) template. All copy is placeholder (dummy) text and exists only to show the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Put the event title here, a one-line subtitle, and a meta row at the bottom. Wrap one word in em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, one shared road ahead.
slides.notes: Team slide. Instead of photos it uses initials in a colored circle, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Demand, drivetrains, and the biggest bottlenecks.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Big heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Steel and rubber still matter, but value now lives in software, sensors, and the network behind them.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: big heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter opener.

items: share of EV models planned across the lineup by 2028
cut from the recall-to-fix cycle with over-the-air updates
lower warranty cost after predictive diagnostics
bullets: Measure impact on the road, not lines of spec written.
Start where the data and the customer pain already overlap.
A small pilot with a clear metric beats a big plan without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a big number, and a label, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The fleet improves without a single visit to the shop.
bullets: Telemetry from drivetrain, brakes, and battery.
Which patterns came before past failures.
A signed update to the right vehicles overnight.
That every car installed it safely.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself.

slides.lead: Same software platform, two routes and two cost curves.
cards: Buy the stack whole. Fast to start, hard to differentiate.
Own every layer. Costly, but yours to shape.
Share a platform with a partner. Balanced risk.
Core in-house, edge features from suppliers.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparisons or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of a connected vehicle.
cards: Decisions made in the car, close to the sensor.
Live view of every vehicle on the road.
Flags odd behavior before the driver feels it.
Signed updates pushed to the whole fleet.
Each car authenticated and the link encrypted.
People who understand the systems and rely on them.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Battery gains slow, so the win shifts to software and cost.
Autonomy features improve fast and reshape the cabin.
The car designs its own driving policy, not just runs it.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a title and a small caption.

slides.quote: The winner is not the carmaker with the most sensors, but the one that turns their signal into a fix the fastest.
slides.by: Sample quote to show the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget under deadline.
bullets: Every feature carries the name of a person who answers for it.
You know up front how success will be judged.
On safety-critical steps a person always decides.
slides.notes: A plain slide with bullets and a strong lead word. Text scales up automatically to fill the space.

slides.lead: A pilot without a metric and an owner ends up in a drawer.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one big sentence centered on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month, and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch into the tool and build a daily overview live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or a break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break, or questions.
Closing slide styled like the title. Contact and a call to action.
